title: Schöneberg: Quick Summary
items: **Schöneberg is four separate corners, not one walk:** Nollendorfplatz and the market, the Akazienkiez, the Bayerisches Viertel, and Rathaus Schöneberg. Pick one per visit; the U-Bahn (U1, U2, U3, U4, U7) connects them.

**Winterfeldtmarkt is the highlight.** Saturdays 8:00 to 16:00, around 250 stalls, Berlin's biggest weekly market. Wednesday is a much smaller morning version. No market on other days or public holidays.

**Most famous addresses are just a plaque on a private building.** Bowie's flat at Hauptstraße 155, Isherwood's room at Nollendorfstraße 17 and Marlene Dietrich's birthplace on Leberstraße have nothing to enter.

**The one curated indoor stop is free:** the Wir waren Nachbarn exhibition inside Rathaus Schöneberg, open 10:00 to 18:00, closed Fridays. Kennedy spoke in front of this building on 26 June 1963, and the Freedom Bell still rings at noon.
**The Bayerisches Viertel memorial costs nothing and needs no ticket:** 80 signs on lamp posts around Bayerischer Platz, each carrying a real anti-Jewish law from 1933 to 1945. Give it an hour.
**Skip the Gasometer viewpoint idea.** The roof terrace is private event space with no tickets or opening hours. Nollendorfplatz is about 10 to 12 minutes from Alexanderplatz on the U2.
